footnotes.text: Apparently Mary, who at this time was married to St. Joseph, was also obliged to be registered.
According to the Mosaic Law, a mother was unclean for seven days after the birth of a son and then had to remain at home for another thirty-three days.

With the completion of his thirteenth year a young Jew became "a son of the Law" and was obliged to the observance of the entire Law. Jewish parents familiarized their sons with the major duties of the Law a year or two before this obligation began.

As God, our Lord has infinite knowledge; as man, He had from the beginning the greatest possible infused knowledge and also the beatific vision. His human mind, however, could advance in experimental knowledge, which is only acquired through the medium of mental faculties and bodily senses.
